vulnerabilities.description: strdup(username) return value is not checked for NULL; a failed heap allocation leaves newUser->username as NULL, causing a NULL pointer dereference on later access.
If strdup(password) fails after strdup(username) succeeded, newUser->username and newUser are leaked because there is no cleanup path for partial allocation failure.
user->username, user->password, and user are freed but the caller's pointer is not cleared, leaving a dangling pointer in the caller that enables use-after-free.
user and user->username are dereferenced without a NULL check; passing a freed or NULL pointer causes a NULL pointer dereference crash.
